This is the perfect webinar, the framework and kind of how this whole thing fits in. If you understand this, it's kind of a high level view. So I'll kind of go through the different parts. First off, there is the introduction where the goal is to build rapport, help them identify with you, get them, all these kind of things happen initially. Then we move over here to the content section, which is where we bring out the one thing. We tell our origin story, we transition secret number one, secret number two, secret number three, and this is the content. This typically takes about an hour to do. Then from there, we transition to the stack and closes, which is this part right here. So we're going to go through this. Now one thing I want to preface, sometimes when people get in and say, they forget the core lesson we talked about today or yesterday, I'm not even sure anymore, that we talked about earlier about the one thing. The presentation, the goal is not try to convince them of a thousand things, it comes back into the one thing. So Jason Flatton taught me this. He said, the idea is to have a single point of belief that your message is built around and emphasize over and over and over again from a variety of different angles. So we're still coming back to the one thing. There's just one thing we got to convince them of, which is your big domino statement. So here's your one thing. If I can get them to believe this, then they have to give me money. So then it comes back to this and then all the other things, all the stories, everything we're doing are all the things that are making them believe this. It's all these different things that are coming back to this one thing. So don't think you're trying to convince them of this and this and this and this. As soon as you start doing the and and and and, it gets all sorts of, it makes the offer muddy, it makes it hard to convert. It always comes back down to one thing. It's having one idea, one single point of belief that we got to get them to believe and we're emphasizing it from whole bunch of different directions. And that's what, if we do it right, knock down the big domino, boom, they have belief and then they join our culture. That's how it works. Cool. Any question about that? It should make sense. All right. So there it is again. And we're going to go through. So did everybody have this on your computer? We downloaded it. So as you go through, when you guys do have a chance to read the expert secrets book, you'll be able to see each of these is kind of sketched out. And so we kind of broke it down in here. When you're doing a webinar, like there's one thing, when I'm in a stage for people, like I can get them reacting really, really easily. So like, what I need to do is I have to get people to say yes over and over and over again. So the back store behind that, I learned from Ted Thomas. If you guys Google Ted Thomas like closing, there's videos around YouTube where he'll be like a Robert Kiyosaki event and he'll have a line of people, like 200 people following him with their credit cards in hand and they're all waving it. And he's like, come on back. And he walks to the back of the room and they call him the Pied Piper of Sales because everyone's doing it. So I was in an event like this and I was speaking and I was selling and I looked in the back and I saw Ted Thomas and I was like, this has got awkward. Like I'm pitching and like the dude is like right there and I did my thing and afterwards he's like, Russell, I want to take you to lunch. I'm like, okay. So we go there and he starts asking me all these questions and we're going through lunch, we're talking and he's like, what are you doing right now? I'm like, what do you mean? He's like, look at your head. And I was like, oh, it's moving around. He's like, you've been nodding since we started talking. I'm like, okay. And he's like, when I was in your room, he's like, what happened? And I was looking in the back of the room and you were doing a great presentation, but everybody was sitting there with their heads like this. He's like, the reason why I closed so well is I ask people to say yes thousands of times my presentation from the second I start my presentation. I'm asking them all these questions. He calls them trial closes to get them to say yes. He's like, watch when I'm speaking, everybody's heads in the room the entire time. It's like a C of P heads bobbing the whole time. For example, one time I was interviewing a guy and he was, and before I interviewed him, he's like, Hey, I'm going to send you my bio so you can read it before you introduce me. And I'm like, okay. So you have to brag about yourself in a way that's like nonchalant or just kind of like, you're doing it, but like you don't look like a jerk. Alright so up to this point so far you guys, just from like a timing standpoint, everything you've done at this point should take about 15 minutes, okay? I've done the title, I've built some rapport, I've done the ruler of the domino statement, I've qualified myself, I told my origin story, I've liked it, I've given them a case study, that's about 15 minutes to get that part done. Okay, so from a timing standpoint, now I'm in a transition to, so what I just did that this whole first thing is, this is my first shot, pushed down the big domino, and some people getting like they fell and they're sold and everything else, they're just getting more and more excited by the end, they're like can you just give me the payment link? I can get those, you guys get those? I get those all the time. People are like can you just give me the link to buy, like I am so sold, I just want this, what's the link? And they're freaking out, so like we'll just send it through the chat to people, and it's really, really fun, we'll close in deals before we get to the pitch, right? So that's the initial goal, it's knock down the big domino, but then the rest of the people are like oh this is awesome, oh wait, hold on, they start freaking out like, and all of a sudden all these things start blocking, right? The vehicle, false beliefs, the internal and the external. So that's when we transition out to the content teaching section of the presentation. Now the biggest thing, and I think most of you guys understand this, but when new people first learn this, the hardest thing they always do is that they want to shift into teacher mode, okay? And they're like oh I'm gonna go teach people all these cool things, and it's like understanding that the way you are teaching people is not through like let me teach you, step one you're gonna do this, step two, that's what all of us as educators want to do, we want to get into the teaching mode, that is the fastest way to destroy yourselves, okay? You're teaching people by saying you have a false belief pattern, you have this thing that's wrong, this is the story you're telling yourself, this is the new story I'm gonna tell you to try to replace that, and what's interesting is that oftentimes you as the teacher, the person leading this, by the time it's done you're like I didn't feel like I taught these guys anything, right? Cause you didn't teach them like any step by step what you do is you completely transform their belief patterns, which for somebody who's experiencing that, is worth a thousand times more than any of like step one you're gonna do this, and step two you transition everything that they've held, they've believed at this point in their life that realize it's not actually correct, and this is the truth, that's where all these huge things happen, that's where people have the big aha moments and they actually transform their lives, okay? And then in the content you do the teaching, all that kind of stuff, but if you bring all the teaching here, which is like a lot of you guys asking me your default, it's switching to teacher mode, in fact most webinars that you guys have done that have failed, in circles of people brought to me, all I do really quick is I scrubbed forward to the teaching section, I watched them within like five seconds, I'm like you're teaching, that's not how you get people to change, okay? When they give you money then you can teach them, we're trying to break their false belief patterns, and make them, and like give them this shift, that's how we're teaching them at this point, okay? So at first it's gonna be weird for some of you guys, but as soon as you do that and you get the feedback, I always get people like, when I teach a good thing and I have tons of stuff, they're like, oh that was awesome, I got a lot out of it and then they don't buy, when I give them this transformational shift, dude that changed my life. I'm like really, I didn't teach them anything, they're like, no, you, I thought this is the way that the world worked, and now I understand it works like this, and that opens up all these possibilities, because that's what we're trying to do through this, is open their mind to the possibilities, and you're not gonna feel like it's teaching them, but they will, because it's gonna be such a big transformation for them, okay? All right, so this is where I'm introducing my three secrets. Luckily all of you guys have already created this slide, we should have wrote it out earlier today. All right, so, and then I move right on. So that's kinda how I do that one. So let's take two minutes and plug in your three secrets that you guys created earlier today. One thing that took our webinar from terrible to converting and doing really, really well is I was teaching too much and then I realized like there still should be curiosity to the how, so with your secrets, you're telling them really why they should do it, not the how they should do it, because the how is your product. So like, you should eat carbs because, here's why you should eat carbs, but I'm not telling them how to do it, like I'm not telling them how to work into their day or what their plan should look like. I'm telling them why they should lift weights, but I'm not giving them a plan to lift weights, I'm just like, they're saying, okay, I need to lift weights, but how do I do it? Right, and they still don't know. So that's actually where your product falls. And when I started thinking of that, I started teaching less and just like leaving them on a cliffhanger more, because they're like, now I have to lift weights, but I don't know how to lift weights, right? And then that's when your product comes in. Yeah, exactly. So that's what really helped me just teach as less as possible. Awesome, thank you. I love that. So you're teaching the why, these stories are just explaining the why, so the product can teach them the how. Okay, let's transition now. So I'm looking, 15 minutes, 15, 15, 15, and then I'm like, okay, I got everything, I've broken all these false belief patterns, like now they're spotless, like, God, the whole world that I've always viewed it, is different, and they're excited, and they're pumped up, and all these good things are happening, but then they don't know what to do, and this is where we start taking, like, hey, I wanna, like, now that you view the world, it's kinda like, Matrix is like, red pill, green pill, it's like, you just took the green pill, right, and it's like, wow, but now what? Okay, and like, the worst thing you could possibly do is not sell somebody at that point. Okay, so everyone's got this weird thing with it, like, oh, I don't wanna sell anything, like, if you care about your people, like, you need to sell them something, because they're gonna see the world in a whole different way, and have no tools or actually ability to implement it, and if you don't give them something at that point, like, you just kinda screw them over, and sometimes there's some, I don't think in this group we have too much of that, but some people have a lot of, like, resistance to the selling, but that's, in my mind, selling is like the highest level of service, like, that's where I'm getting you, and I'm gonna force you to make a financial commitment so you can actually live and get that result that we talked about, because I've painted the picture, like, you're in, you want that, you believe this is a vehicle, but if I don't get you to commit with your wallet, you're never gonna get there, okay? In fact, the section in the book I call your moral obligation, like, this is your, if you honestly believe that the vehicle that you are putting people into is the best thing for their success, that J. Bram said, like, you have a moral obligation to do everything within your power to get people to give you money so they can get in that vehicle so they can get their result. Not about you, I believe my message is 100%, that's why I'm so consistent with it, like, I believe that ClickFunnels, that Funnel, like, this whole thing that we do is, the vehicle that people need to transform the world, so that's why I have no shame in selling this thing like crazy, because I know the effect it's had on people, and so you need to have that same kind of commitment and belief in what you're doing, and if you do, then this car becomes fun and easy. If you don't, it becomes all nervous and like, ah, you'll freak out, but for me, like, this is my favorite part of it. So when I transition, I'm coming through here, and there's always this, like, weird awkwardness when you transition from, like, you doing the teaching to the selling part. So in Brandon yesterday, like, I go into my wife's home parties, she's invited all the home parties, and I always ask if I can tag along, and I want to sit in the back, because I love watching, like, the people doing the home parties, and they do this good, they do this good, and they get all excited, and all of a sudden, they start transitioning to the, to, like, where they're gonna ask people for money, and, like, they get nervous, and they start sweating, and, like, they get, like, all fidgety, and it's, like, the funniest thing in the world, because none of them know how to make the transition point. But if you do it right, like, the transition's really, really easy, so there's kind of two things that I've been doing, one I've been doing for 10 years, one I started doing probably about six months ago in a webinar, and they're the biggest things that make this transition fast. So the first thing I do is this first slide right here, where I come back to all these things I just taught them. I say, I wanna, we've been here for about an hour, and I got a question for you guys. Do you think that if you were to do what we just talked about here, okay, so for me now, I'm plugging back in my three secrets in one slide, if you were to do it, and you found a funnel that actually worked, that's secret number one, funnel hacked, okay, and then you create something similar, and you modeled it, that's secret number two, and then you went and you found the exact same website, your competitors are getting all their traffic from, and you bought ads on the exact same website, secret number three, do you think you could be as successful as they are? And people are like, oh, maybe, and then in the story I talk about Venus Factor, they're making over 100 grand a day, and I'm like, look, I know that, for most years that may seem like far-fetched, like it's even big for me to expect to make 100 grand a day, even to me, I struggle with that, so let's just say you totally screwed up and you only make 10% of what Venus Factor makes, okay, and you only make an extra $10,000 a day, how many guys an extra $10,000 a day would be pretty awesome, and everyone laughs like, yeah, and that's if you screw it up, but let's just say like you're really in confidence it's your first time doing it, and let's say you totally, totally screw up, and you only get 10% of the 10%, that's only an extra 1,000 bucks a day, how many guys with $1,000 a day completely radically transform the quality of your life right now, like dang, I guess $30,000 a month, that's more than most people make an entire year, that's if you totally, totally screw it up, let's just say you're like, I'm a brand new newbie, I don't even know what a funnel is yet, I'm so nervous, and let's say you totally, totally, totally screw up, you only get 10% of the 10%, of the 10%, you only make an extra 100 bucks a day, guess what, three grand a month, what'd you do with three grand a month? You want me to stop and let go? What's better for you guys, do you think? Just keep moving. Okay. All right. So now I've shared the first thing. So now I'm gonna go as I'm gonna go and I'm gonna show my stack slide number one. The psychology behind the stack slide, I learned this initially from Arm and Moran. It has made me more money collectively probably than anything else I've ever done. The first time you do it, you will feel weird and awkward and embarrassed, but you will get over it when you're cashing checks that are bigger than anything you ever dreamt of. So the psychology behind it, when most people speak that haven't learned this process through either me or through Arm and I think only guys have ever taught it, but what most people do is they talk about their offer and they talk about the bonuses and they go through all this stuff. And then the last thing they show bonus like number five or six or 10 or whatever it is, and then they talk about the price. And the problem is our human minds are very, we can remember things not like, it's very short of our memory. What happens is like, here's the best thing I got then bonus number two, number three, number four, and this is my smallest bonus, and then this is the price. And what happens is that your brain associates the price with the last thing it saw. And so if the last thing you offer them isn't worth that much, then they're not gonna buy. And so Arm and taught me, I was out speaking, doing some stuff and he was speaking on a stage, it was a Dan Kennedy stage. Before he got up, he's like, hey man, I wanna show you something. I'm like, what's that? He's like, it's called the stack. I'm like, what is it? He's like, just watch. Then he got up on stage and I'm watching Arm and who's like, one of the first guys I learned tons of this speaking from. He goes through, does this whole pitch and I'm watching and he gets to the stack and it was like, I didn't know what it was. It was this weird thing and it was like, super fag. All this stuff was happening, I didn't know what it was. Then he closed and I saw this room of 1,200 people. I saw more than half of them jump up and run to the back and sign up for his thing. And I'm sitting there back just watching, like doing the math, counting everything. And it was done, I waited for every kind of left and he walked over and he had this big old stack, fast stack of order forms this big. He walked over and he's like, do you see that? I was like, what was that? He's like, it was the stack. He's like, do you want order forms like this? I'm like, yeah, he's like, did you gotta learn the stack? And he kind of walked away. I'm like, dude, what's the stack? He's like, all right, I'll tell you. And he told me what I told you guys about the whole memory thing. He's like, watch what I do. He's like, I have a slide where I talk about my number one thing and I talk about it. And then I stack it, right? So I'll show you the stack slide, which is kind of jacked up because of the keynote thing. So I apologize, it looks ugly, but conceptually, here's the first thing. Then you introduce the second thing, you talk about it and then you come back to the slide. Now you stack the second thing. And the third thing, the fourth thing. And by the time it's done, I have all these things, I stack them all together and that's when I introduce price. Now they're just like, they're rationalizing the price with the entire thing, not just the last thing you talked about, okay? And so you're gonna feel kind of weird. I promise you, do you guys feel weird the first time you did it? It's kind of weird. Yeah, I didn't like it either. In fact, the Armand told me, I was speaking to his event two weeks later. He said, if you speak at my stage, you have to use the stack because I need you to make money. And I was like, all right, so he taught it to me, he sent me his slides, I learned it. I went to that next event, I did the stack and I was the number one seller at his event. Made more money on stage in one time than I ever made prior. And it was cool, I don't know if Brent's here, but Brent came to that event with me and he was sitting there and we got done and everything happened. I had my big stack of order forms. I think I made, it was like $160,000 in sales and I got half that, so like 80 grand was mine to take home. He was like, dude, you made 80 grand in 90 minutes. That's insane. I was like, I know dude, it was the stack, like this is magic. And ever since then, I've never not done, actually it's not true, one time, I like, I'd done the stack a bunch of times it worked and one time I was like kind of embarrassed, I'm like, ah, it's not gonna work, I'm just gonna, and I didn't do it and I blanked on stage. It was the most embarrassing thing in my life and I swore that day I will never, ever give a presentation or I don't do the stack again. So, like it literally, everything I've ever done, like I can contribute more money to this one concept than anything else I've done. So like just use it, it'll be weird, it's the first time and by the second or third time you'll love it and then you'll never wanna leave it away because it's just, it's so worth it. Okay, so talk about my masterclass and I stack it. So that means you can get the six week funnel at Masterclass, total value of $2,997. Okay, and then I introduce now the tool. I'm like, I don't finance things, but I do know someone who will finance it for you. It's called Visa and Mastercard, you ever heard of them? They will finance this whole entire thing for you. And the coolest thing about Visa and Mastercard, in fact, the way I tell it, I was like, when I first got started online, I wanted to buy Mark Joyner as my mentor, I needed a thousand dollar course I wanted to buy it, I didn't have it, I emailed him asking me to finance it, and his assistant rolled back saying, we don't finance things that Visa and Mastercard do. I was like, well how does that work? Like, that's kind of what he said, well what's the minimum monthly payment on a thousand dollar product? I'm like, I don't know, what, 50 bucks? Okay, so you put it on a credit card, Visa and Mastercard funds it, in 30 days from now, you have to pay $50. Like, are you okay in 30 days from now, if you're going through the training, maybe you haven't made any money, you haven't been 30 days, can you afford $50? I'm like, yeah, I can afford 50 bucks. Okay, cool, so you have 30 days, hopefully you made 50 bucks, if not, you pay that, then you got 30 more days to keep going through the course, right, you keep going through the course, keep going through it, and then in 60 days, do you think you'll make 50 bucks? Yeah, I think so. Okay, so if you made 50 bucks, you pay that from the profits you made, and that point forward, your business will be paying for those payments, so it's now free, you paid $50 for this course. Okay, now if you didn't make it after 60 days, what do you think? Can you pay $50 and 60 days from now? Yeah, I think so. Okay, so that gives you 30 more days. The next 30 days, you think then by the, that or your 90 days, it's in the 90 days, do you think you could have, you'd be able to make at least 50 bucks from your business? Well, if not, it's probably my own fault, right? Exactly.
